description_long: This is a futuristic, angular airplane with a distinctive X-shaped wing configuration, reminiscent of science fiction spacecraft. The fuselage is elongated and tapers to a pointed nose, with a cockpit canopy set forward and detailed with dark, transparent panels. Four cylindrical engines are mounted at the rear of the wings, each with visible exhaust ports. The wings are straight and extend outward with prominent red markings and colored tips, while the tail section features a pair of vertical stabilizers integrated into the wing structure. The surface is primarily gray with red and black accents, and the overall design suggests a high-performance, agile craft. The airplane appears to be constructed from advanced composite materials, with a hard, smooth exterior finish.
description: A sleek, gray airplane with an X-shaped wing layout, four rear engines, and red accent markings, featuring a forward cockpit and integrated vertical stabilizers.
description_view_0: Rear view showing four cylindrical engines, the X-shaped wing layout, and the integrated vertical stabilizers.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, forward cockpit canopy, and the straight, extended wings with colored tips.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical X-wing configuration with visible red markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the wing and engine arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with pronounced anhedral angle and extended wingtip elements; distinctive X-shaped configuration.
attribute_engine_type: Four rear-mounted jet engines integrated into the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers integrated into the rear wing structure, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visibly extended; likely retractable landing gear based on design, but not shown in images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or dual-seat based on size.
